mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is trending down, discount rates and refinancing costs fall, so equity valuation multiples expand and the S&P 500 posts positive forward returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the average return in those periods is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation breakout above recent levels forces tighter policy expectations and higher real discount rates, which pressure equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its prior 6-month average by more than 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show negative 20-day forward SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE paired with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which reduces earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined UNRATE-PAYEMS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in periods where UNRATE rises while PAYEMS falls for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp HOUST contraction below trend signals housing demand rollover and weaker future consumption, so the S&P 500 tends to underperform over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day forward returns more frequently than baseline, or if the signal flips positive during repeated housing contractions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS implies stronger nominal retail sales and household spending, which supports revenue growth expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in periods with above-trend RSAFS growth, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while GDPC1 is also expanding, it signals broad real activity and improving corporate volume demand, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-GDPC1 expansion state does not produce higher 60-day forward returns than either expansion series alone, or if the effect disappears in repeated growth episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% raises financing costs and compresses equity valuations, so the S&P 500 should deliver negative returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after rate crossings above 4.5%, or if the trigger loses sign consistency across multiple tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-revision growth confirmation regime, simultaneous strength in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates genuine demand acceleration rather than inventory noise, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS signal does not beat the unconditional return distribution in recovery periods, or if positive returns do not concentrate when both series are accelerating together.
